ideas.intro: Ideas for projects, activities, books, games, and more for students with a high level of interest.

items.content:
[ ] (Folk Dancing) Gather friends and family and teach them the term's dances.
[ ] (Jump Rope) Go see a local jump rope competition (or view one[ online](https://www.youtube.com/live/n7q3ByKc7Jo?feature=share)).
[ ] (Playground Games) Teach friends or neighbors to play the games.
[ ] Explore one of the nutrition books listed below or visit a [farmer's market](https://www.usdalocalfoodportal.com/fe/fdirectory_farmersmarket/?source=fe&directory=farmersmarket&location=&x=&y=) near you.

[ ] (Jump Rope) Make up other exercises to go with the jump rope songs when inclement weather keeps you indoors. [Here](https://activeforlife.com/1-skipping-rope-8-fun-activities/) are a few ideas.
[ ] Make a healthy treat to share with neighbors or take one to the local fire or police station.
[ ] If it is winter where you live, try [these ideas ](https://washingtonparent.com/winter-family-fitness-can-be-fun/)for winter family fitness for all ages.
[ ] (Folk Dancing) Gather friends and family and teach them the term's dances.

[ ] Learn how astronauts eat healthy foods in space at [NASA's website](https://www.nasa.gov/audience/foreducators/stem-on-station/ditl_eating).
[ ] (Jump Rope) Try these Jump for the Moon activities from [NASA](https://www.nasa.gov/sites/default/files/atoms/files/jumpforthemoon_-_student.pdf).
[ ] (Folk Dancing) Gather friends and family and teach them the term's dances.
